episodes.title: Bulls on Course, Valley of the Deals, and CNBC Investigates 7/7/26
episodes.summary:
**Summary**
A broad US markets show covering a tech-led equity pullback (Samsung earnings, SpaceX's NASDAQ 100 inclusion), Amazon's $25bn bond raise, Sun Valley media deal chatter, Walmart grocery price cuts, and a CNBC investigation into blacklisted Chinese LiDAR maker HSI. Most direct relevance to an EM trader comes through Fed/rate-cut expectations tied to falling oil and softening CPI, and continued mega-cap credit issuance signaling tighter corporate liquidity dynamics.

**Key points**
- Nasdaq/semis fell ~5% in 3 of the last 4 sessions after Samsung's profit jump disappointed elevated expectations; SOX index back to mid-June levels after a +90% YTD run.
- Bespoke's Paul Hickey: doesn't expect the Fed to hike or cut before year-end, but says inflation appears to have peaked — crude oil's two-month decline and a falling ISM prices-paid index point to a flat-to-negative month-over-month CPI in next week's report.
- Walmart is rolling out summer grocery price cuts (ground beef -12%, private label and Coca-Cola/PepsiCo/Frito-Lay items) after its worst quarter in four years; Kroger testing similar cuts.
- Amazon is raising $25bn in bonds for AI/data-center capex, joining Google, Oracle, Meta and SpaceX in heavy tech bond issuance this year; Amazon expects negative free cash flow in 2026.
- CNBC investigation: Shanghai-based HSI, blacklisted by the Pentagon for alleged Chinese military ties, still supplies LiDAR sensors to Nvidia/Amazon Zoox-linked autonomous platforms at roughly half the cost of US competitors, raising data-security concerns given China's legal power to compel data access.
- US housing market survey shows buyer leverage rising (44% of agents report balanced market vs 30% a year ago); Sunbelt markets softening.

**FX / rates implications**
- Softer oil and a potentially cooler US CPI print reduce the odds of Fed hawkish surprises, supportive for broad EM carry (INR, IDR, PHP, MXN, BRL) via lower UST yields and a steadier-to-softer DXY.
- Falling oil is a tailwind for oil-importing EM Asia currencies (INR, IDR, PHP) via improved terms of trade and current-account dynamics, while a negative for LATAM oil exporters' (COP, MXN via Pemex-adjacent flows) terms of trade.
- Heavy US mega-cap bond issuance (Amazon, Google, Oracle, Meta, SpaceX) could tighten global credit conditions and compete for dollar liquidity, a mild headwind for EM credit spreads and hard-currency sovereign issuance.
- US-China tech/security friction (HSI blacklist scrutiny) keeps trade and tech-decoupling risk alive, a background negative for CNY/CNH and broader Asia FX risk sentiment.

**Watch list**
- Next week's US CPI print — key catalyst for Fed rate-cut repricing and UST yields/DXY direction.
- PepsiCo earnings Thursday for read-through on consumer/grocery pricing and input-cost trends.
- Further mega-cap bond issuance and its effect on credit spreads and dollar funding costs.
- SpaceX share-lockup expirations and Russell/NASDAQ rebalancing flows as a gauge of broader risk appetite.
